message:
fix(validation): flexible serde deserialization + case-insensitive status matching

Harden the ext_full_validation pipeline against JSON format variations
encountered in real-world conformance and onboarding reports.

Serde changes: Replace blanket #[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")] on all
report structs with per-field #[serde(alias = "camelCase")] annotations.
This accepts both snake_case and camelCase field names, making the pipeline
resilient to format evolution without breaking existing reports. Affected
structs: OnboardingQueueDoc, OnboardingQueueEntry, ShardReport, ShardResult,
DossierRecord, AutoRepairSummary, AutoRepairExtension, RepairEventRecord,
ScenarioCounts, ScenarioResult, ProviderCompatReport, ProviderFailure,
ProviderCompatEvent.

Onboarding queue: Add top_500/top_300/top_100 alternative arrays with
into_entries() fallback chain. Make rank, pi_relevant, and
pi_relevance_score optional for forward-compatibility with evolving queue
formats.

Status matching: Use eq_ignore_ascii_case() for all status comparisons
and accept "passed"/"failed"/"skipped" variants alongside "pass"/"fail"/
"skip" in conformance and scenario results. Normalize provider_mode to
lowercase and deduplicate provider failure entries.

Rendering: Escape markdown table cell content (pipe characters) to prevent
malformed table output when extension IDs or classification reasons contain
pipe characters.

Provider compat: Handle missing provider_compat_report.json gracefully
(already returned Ok with None, but refactored to unify event-based
augmentation path).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
